original_image_tags: The left third of the document shows faint, reversed text bleeding through from the opposite side of the thin paper. The paper has vertical fold lines and standard three-hole punched binder holes along the top edge. The bleed-through appears to be typed text, but is illegible from this side.
The right third of the document shows very faint, ghost-like impressions of text from the other side. The paper is slightly darker and more textured here.

The full document is a single sheet of light tan, aged paper. It is folded into thirds vertically, creating two prominent creases. There are five holes punched along the top edge for a ring binder. The paper shows minor wear and foxing, consistent with archival storage. The only clearly legible text on this side are the two struck-through "CONFIDENTIAL" stamps at the top and bottom centers, and the small handwritten "Incl/ 2". All other text is faint bleed-through from the reverse side.

elements.description: A crossed-out 'CONFIDENTIAL' stamp at the top center of the page. Handwritten text 'Incl 2' in the center. A second crossed-out 'CONFIDENTIAL' stamp near the center right. Faint reversed text and diagrams are visible bleeding through from the reverse side of the page.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on the presence of two crossed-out 'CONFIDENTIAL' stamps, the handwritten 'Incl 2' text, and faint bleed-through text, but they differ on the exact number and location of binder holes (three vs. five) and on the presence of visible diagrams in the bleed-through.
